mnemonics: Most Constrained Non-Convex Problems Need Experts
keyPoints: The Limited-Information Method (LIM) and Conjugate Gradient methods can work well for non-convex problems but are limited to specific conditions.
Descent-based methods like gradient descent and quasi-Newton methods can converge to local minima, which may not be the global minimum.
The use of penalty methods, such as adding a penalty term to the objective function, can help to ensure convergence to the global minimum.
